hero.title: Hairspray
hero.reason: In honor of Ballpoint Pen Day, Hairspray is a fun choice because the movie features the iconic dance moves and colorful style of the 1960s, much like the bold and vibrant lines created with ballpoint pens.
hero.blurb: Hairspray is a fun musical comedy with great dance numbers and a star-studded cast. The film's themes of self-acceptance and empowerment make it a great choice for any day, but especially one that celebrates creativity and expression.

released.title: Mr. Brooks
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Mr. Brooks is a psychological thriller that explores the darker side of human nature, making it a great choice for fans of suspenseful films.
released.blurb: Mr. Brooks is a gripping thriller with a standout performance from Kevin Costner. The film's twists and turns will keep you on the edge of your seat, making it a great choice for anyone who loves a good mystery.

birthday.title: Sister Act
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to Judy Brown, but since no prominent actor's birthday is on this day, we chose an alternative: 'Sister Act' stars Judy's near-namesake, Judy 'Judge' Reinhold is not in this, however, Whoopi Goldberg, born not on this day but in July is our star so we instead celebrate with a Whoopi Goldberg classic: Happy birthday to nearby-born actor, but actually, we just love Whoopi: Whoopi is not the birthday but Sister Act star Whoopi does star.
birthday.blurb: Sister Act is a hilarious comedy with a great cast, including Whoopi Goldberg. The film's music and dancing make it a fun choice for anyone who loves a good musical.

awful.title: I Know Who Killed Me
awful.reason: This movie is infamous for its poor plot, acting, and overall production, making it a prime example of a dumpster dive film.
awful.blurb: I Know Who Killed Me is a cringe-worthy thriller with a convoluted plot and subpar acting. The film's attempts at suspense and drama fall flat, making it a great example of a movie that's so bad, it's good.
